dependencies.description: Requires IP reachability between VRRP peers on the same subnet
dependencies.name: IP connectivity

dependencies.description: All VRRP routers for a given VRID must share a common subnet
dependencies.name: Same subnet

dependencies.description: All routers in the VRRP group must be configured with the same Virtual Router ID and virtual IP address
dependencies.name: Matching VRID and virtual IP

failure_modes.description: Both routers claim MASTER for the same VRID, causing duplicate virtual IP and traffic disruption

resolutions.action: Check L2 connectivity between VRRP peers: verify VLAN, trunk, and switchport configuration
Check L2 connectivity between VRRP peers: verify VLAN, trunk, and switchport configuration

root_causes.cause: L2 issue preventing advertisements from reaching the other router

symptoms: show vrrp on both routers shows MASTER state
ARP table on hosts shows MAC flapping for the virtual IP
Intermittent connectivity for hosts using the virtual gateway

failure_modes.description: Master fails but backup does not transition to MASTER, causing total gateway outage

resolutions.action: Verify VRRP is configured on backup: 'show vrrp' on backup router
Verify VRRP is configured on backup: 'show vrrp' on backup router
Verify VRRP is configured on backup: 'show vrrp' on backup router
Check VRID matches on both sides
Check VRID matches on both sides
Check VRID matches on both sides
Verify virtual IP address matches on both routers
Verify virtual IP address matches on both routers
Verify virtual IP address matches on both routers
Check for L2 loops that might be forwarding stale advertisements
Check for L2 loops that might be forwarding stale advertisements
Check for L2 loops that might be forwarding stale advertisements
Verify interface is up on backup router

Verify interface is up on backup router

root_causes.cause: VRRP not configured on backup router
Advertisements still arriving (L2 loop forwarding old packets)
Wrong virtual router ID (VRID mismatch between peers)
master_down_interval too high (delayed failover appears as no failover)

symptoms: Virtual IP becomes unreachable after master failure
Backup router remains in BACKUP state despite master being down

failure_modes.description: Failover occurs but takes too long, causing noticeable traffic disruption

resolutions.action: Reduce adver-interval for faster failover (VRRPv3 supports centisecond values)
Reduce adver-interval for faster failover (VRRPv3 supports centisecond values)
Reduce adver-interval for faster failover (VRRPv3 supports centisecond values)
Configure preemption if higher-priority router should reclaim MASTER role
Configure preemption if higher-priority router should reclaim MASTER role
Configure preemption if higher-priority router should reclaim MASTER role
Configure interface tracking to decrement priority on upstream link failure, triggering faster failover
Configure interface tracking to decrement priority on upstream link failure, triggering faster failover
Configure interface tracking to decrement priority on upstream link failure, triggering faster failover

root_causes.cause: No preemption configured (higher-priority router does not reclaim MASTER after recovery)
Interface tracking not configured for upstream failure detection

symptoms: Ping loss during failover exceeds expected duration
Failover takes 3+ seconds with default timers

messages.description: Announces master status and virtual router parameters
messages.name: VRRP Advertisement

purpose: Virtual Router Redundancy Protocol — provides default gateway redundancy with standards-based failover

states.description: Standby, monitoring master advertisements. Steady state when this router is intended to be the backup.
states.name: BACKUP

states.description: Active, forwarding traffic for virtual IP
states.name: MASTER

timers.name: adver-interval
timers.tuning_advice: Lower for faster failover detection. VRRPv3 supports centisecond granularity for sub-second failover.

timers.description: Time before backup declares master failed
timers.name: master_down_interval
timers.tuning_advice: Derived from adver-interval; tune adver-interval instead. Formula: (3 x adver-interval) + skew_time.

timers.description: Priority-based offset to stagger failover. Lower priority = longer wait, ensuring higher priority backup becomes master first.
timers.name: skew_time
timers.tuning_advice: Not directly configurable. Controlled by priority assignment. Ensures deterministic master election among multiple backups.

transitions.trigger: Priority is 255 (IP owner) or master_down_interval expires with no master advertisement

transitions.failure_if_stuck: Master still alive but not reachable (split-brain possible)

notes.note: VRRPv3 supported. 'fhrp version vrrp v3' to enable VRRPv3 mode.
VRRPv3 natively supported under 'router vrrp'. Interface-level configuration.
VRRPv3 supported. 'feature vrrp' required. Supports object tracking for priority decrement.
